commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9a93839a2c43439eb563ab0d6f0e247ddb4bac7d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a strict tool mode config/env surface plus embedded-agent tool-call normalization, replay diagnostics, read-alias rejection, dotenv blocking, and tests.

PR surface: Source +264, Tests +626. Total +890 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR blockers: source and diff inspection show the boolean-only contract, missing config baseline update, branch conflict, and strict mode not reaching malformed argument repair or transport argument coercion. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer review is needed because the PR lacks contributor real-behavior proof, is conflicting, and needs a contract/upgrade decision rather than a narrow automated repair.

target.title: Add strict tool mode diagnostics and report contracts
target.body:
Implements strict tool mode plumbing across embedded runs, transport streams, and OpenAI-compatible gateway responses.

Changes include:

Adds strict/warn/off tool strictness handling for tool argument repairs, alias repairs, and tool-name normalization.
Surfaces structured toolStrictnessReport / tool_strictness_report diagnostics.
Wires config/runtime strictness mode into embedded agent runs and provider transports.
Adds mock/no-API test coverage plus testing docs for real API validation.
Updates config baseline hash for the new config surface.
Verification:

pnpm config:docs:check
pnpm check
pnpm build
Targeted strict-tool-mode tests
src/channels/plugins/read-only.test.ts rerun with low concurrency
Note: full default-parallel pnpm test hit local no-output timeouts across unrelated shards; the failed shard passed when rerun with OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1.
target.author: jasonzhangtianrui

commands.author: saju01

target.title: fix(github-copilot): prefer live model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- prefer GitHub Copilot's live `/models` catalog rows for visible model metadata
- keep bounded static Copilot rows as a degraded fallback when live discovery is disabled, unauthenticated, or unavailable
- merge live + fallback rows by model id with live metadata winning on duplicates, so stale fallback rows cannot mask live context windows/reasoning/input data
- preserve explicit user config as the operator override path
- add regression coverage for retained fallback rows, discovery-disabled fallback behavior, and live-first duplicate handling

Fixes #88548.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/github-copilot/models.test.ts extensions/github-copilot/index.test.ts extensions/github-copilot/provider-policy-api.test.ts src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts src/commands/model-picker.test.ts`
  - 154 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/github-copilot`
  - 0 warnings, 0 errors

Note: `pnpm exec oxlint ...` attempted to fetch optional platform packages and hit local VM disk pressure (`ERR_PNPM_ENOSPC`, filesystem ~98% full). The local checked-in `node_modules/.bin/oxlint` binary was present and completed successfully.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** GitHub Copilot model visibility and metadata now use live `/models` rows first, while retaining bounded static fallback rows only for degraded discovery/offline cases. Duplicate ids keep the live row, preventing stale fallback metadata such as old GPT-5.5 context windows from masking live values.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS/Darwin arm64, Node v22.22.2, pnpm v11.2.2, authenticated GitHub CLI token for a real Copilot-enabled account. The live Copilot API base URL resolved to `https://api.enterprise.githubcopilot.com`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
GH_TOKEN="$(gh auth token)" pnpm exec tsx -e '
import { resolveCopilotApiToken } from "./extensions/github-copilot/token.ts";
import { fetchCopilotModelCatalog } from "./extensions/github-copilot/models.ts";
async function main() {
  const githubToken = process.env.GH_TOKEN;
  if (!githubToken) throw new Error("missing GH_TOKEN from gh auth");
  const token = await resolveCopilotApiToken({ githubToken, env: process.env });
  const rows = await fetchCopilotModelCatalog({ copilotApiToken: token.token, baseUrl: token.baseUrl });
  const interesting = rows.filter((row) => ["gpt-5.5", "claude-opus-4.7-1m-internal", "gpt-5.4", "gpt-5.3-codex"].includes(row.id));
  console.log(JSON.stringify({
    proof: "patched github-copilot fetchCopilotModelCatalog live /models",
    baseUrl: token.baseUrl,
    modelCount: rows.length,
    selected: interesting.map((row) => ({
      id: row.id,
      name: row.name,
      api: row.api,
      reasoning: row.reasoning,
      contextWindow: row.contextWindow,
      maxTokens: row.maxTokens,
      input: row.input,
      compat: row.compat,
      thinkingLevelMap: row.thinkingLevelMap,
    })),
    firstFiveIds: rows.slice(0, 5).map((row) => row.id),
  }, null, 2));
}
main().catch((err) => { console.error(err instanceof Error ? err.message : err); process.exit(1); });
'
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from the command above. Token values are never printed; the rows below are from the live `/models` response through the patched `fetchCopilotModelCatalog` path.

```json
{
  "proof": "patched github-copilot fetchCopilotModelCatalog live /models",
  "baseUrl": "https://api.enterprise.githubcopilot.com",
  "modelCount": 36,
  "selected": [
    {
      "id": "claude-opus-4.7-1m-internal",
      "name": "Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)(Internal only)",
      "api": "anthropic-messages",
      "reasoning": true,
      "contextWindow": 1000000,
      "maxTokens": 64000,
      "input": ["text", "image"],
      "compat": {
        "supportedReasoningEfforts": ["low", "medium", "high", "xhigh", "max"]
      },
      "thinkingLevelMap": { "xhigh": "xhigh" }
    },
    {
      "id": "gpt-5.3-codex",
      "name": "GPT-5.3-Codex",
      "api": "openai-responses",
      "reasoning": true,
      "contextWindow": 400000,
      "maxTokens": 128000,
      "input": ["text", "image"],
      "compat": {
        "supportedReasoningEfforts": ["low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "gpt-5.4",
      "name": "GPT-5.4",
      "api": "openai-responses",
      "reasoning": true,
      "contextWindow": 1050000,
      "maxTokens": 128000,
      "input": ["text", "image"],
      "compat": {
        "supportedReasoningEfforts": ["none", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "gpt-5.5",
      "name": "GPT-5.5",
      "api": "openai-responses",
      "reasoning": true,
      "contextWindow": 1050000,
      "maxTokens": 128000,
      "input": ["text", "image"],
      "compat": {
        "supportedReasoningEfforts": ["none", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"]
      }
    }
  ],
  "firstFiveIds": [
    "claude-opus-4.6-1m",
    "claude-opus-4.6",
    "claude-opus-4.7-1m-internal",
    "claude-opus-4.7-high",
    "claude-opus-4.7-xhigh"
  ]
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The patched live catalog path returned 36 Copilot models from the real `/models` endpoint. GPT-5.5 is present with live metadata (`contextWindow: 1050000`, `maxTokens: 128000`, reasoning enabled), and Claude Opus 4.7 1M is present with live Anthropic transport, 1M context, xhigh/max compat, and the xhigh thinking map. Unit coverage now confirms the provider catalog appends fallback rows after live rows and dedupes duplicate ids so live metadata wins.
- **What was not tested:** Full built `node openclaw.mjs models list --provider github-copilot` from this source checkout was not completed because earlier `pnpm build` hung in `tsdown` at 0% CPU in this local checkout. The patched Copilot token/catalog modules were exercised directly against the live Copilot service instead.

Maintainer compatibility note: if Copilot live discovery is disabled, unauthenticated, or unavailable, this PR now keeps the bounded static fallback catalog visible. When live discovery succeeds, live rows are listed first and fallback rows are appended only for ids absent from the live response.


---

## Update: live auto-detection also unlocks 1M context + reasoning for the latest models

Adding a fresh, real-account datapoint that makes the value of this PR concrete: **auto-detecting live metadata isn't just about visibility — it surfaces the correct context window, max output, and reasoning support that static rows silently throttle.**

On an entitled account, the live Copilot `/models` endpoint reports `claude-opus-4.8` as:

```
claude-opus-4.8 ->
  max_context_window_tokens: 1000000
  max_prompt_tokens:          936000
  max_output_tokens:          64000
  supports.adaptive_thinking: true   (thinking budget 1024–32000)
  supports.reasoning_effort:  [low, medium, high, xhigh, max]
```

Before this PR, the static catalog row pinned `claude-opus-4.8` to a 200K window (~128–164K usable) with no reasoning, so `openclaw models list` showed `164k/195k` and sessions ran capped — despite full 1M entitlement. This is the same 1M window the VS Code Copilot **"long context" toggle** exposes; it is **not** a separate model id, just the full window of `claude-opus-4.8`, so live `/models` metadata is the only correct source for it.

With this PR's live-wins-on-duplicate merge, the live row wins on the duplicate id and the entitled 1M window + reasoning are surfaced automatically — no per-model static bump required.

**Why this matters for scope:** PR #91875 / issue #91869 patch this for `claude-opus-4.8` by manually editing static metadata. This PR removes the need for those one-off bumps entirely: every current and future Copilot model auto-inherits its live context window, max output, vision, and reasoning capabilities. The 4.8 → 1M case is simply the most recent example of static metadata silently capping an entitled model.

Related: #91875, #91869.
